title: Global Stories: Wars, Pets, and Recoveries
summary: Today, we dive into the human cost of conflict in Iran, a new pet law in Brazil, US political changes, a celebrity lawsuit update, tragic crime in NYC, and a retrieved ancient artifact.
script:
Hello everyone, and welcome back to the show! It's great to have you with us today as we look at some important stories from around the world and right here at home. We've got a lot to talk about, from serious global events to heartwarming legal changes, and even a mystery solved. So, let's jump right in and break down the news. 

We'll start with some very concerning news coming out of Iran. For about a month now, there has been ongoing conflict. This war is having a really tough impact on ordinary people. News reports tell us that folks from different parts of Iran are describing a lot of struggles. They talk about what they call 'expanding strikes,' which are making their daily lives very difficult. Beyond the strikes, there is a lot of economic pain. This means it's harder for people to live and make money. On top of that, there's a strong fear of repression, which means people are worried about their safety and freedom. One person even said they haven't slept for days. This really shows us the mounting desperation that people are feeling in Iran after just a month of war. It's a very challenging situation for many, and their stories paint a clear picture of the difficulties they face every single day.

Adding to the tough situation in Iran, we have news about a major economic blow. The country's two largest steel plants have been forced to shut down. This information comes directly from the companies themselves. The reason for these shutdowns? Air strikes. Iran claims these strikes were first launched by Israel. They also say that Israel acted in co-ordination with the United States. These strikes, Iran says, happened last week. The shutting down of these two very important steel plants could cause really big damage to the Iranian economy. Steel production is a key part of any country's economy, so losing this capacity will hurt a lot. This adds to the economic pain that ordinary Iranians are already feeling, as we just discussed. It's clear that the ongoing conflict is having serious and direct effects on the country's industry and its people's livelihoods.

Now, let's talk about the political side of this conflict. French President Emmanuel Macron has spoken out about the way the war against Iran is being handled. Specifically, he criticised the approach taken by former US President Trump. Macron's comments were quite direct. He said, "Be serious... don't speak every day." These words were seen as a clear jab at the US leader. The criticism pointed to Trump's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran. Macron suggested that a more consistent and serious approach might be better in such a complex and sensitive situation. This shows that even among allied nations, there can be different views on how to manage international conflicts and how leaders should communicate about them. It highlights the importance of consistent communication, especially when dealing with such serious global issues.

Moving on to some news from the world of US politics. We learned that former US President Trump has removed US Attorney General Pam Bondi from her position. Her time as America's top law enforcement officer was marked by some difficult challenges. One specific issue overshadowed her period in office: the justice department's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. The way the department managed this high-profile and very serious case became a significant point of discussion and criticism. The removal of a key figure like the Attorney General always sparks conversation and raises questions about the reasons behind such decisions. This particular removal brings up memories of the Epstein investigation and how its handling created difficulties for those in charge of the justice system.

Next, we have an update from Hollywood involving a well-known actress. A judge has made a decision in the harassment lawsuit brought by Blake Lively against Justin Baldoni. The judge dismissed most of Blake Lively's claims in the suit. This means many of the things she had accused Baldoni of have been thrown out of court. However, the case is not completely over. The judge did leave in place three specific allegations against Baldoni. This is important because it means the civil trial will still go ahead. The trial is scheduled to begin next month. So, while a large part of the case has been dismissed, there are still some serious points that will be argued in court. We will be watching to see what happens when the trial starts.

Let's shift gears to some positive news, this time from Brazil. Lawmakers in the Brazilian Congress have introduced a new law that addresses the beloved pets of separated couples. This new law grants joint custody over pets. This means that when a couple breaks up, they can both share the responsibility and time with their furry friends. The lawmakers see this change in the law as a direct reflection of how important pets are to their owners. For many people, pets are not just animals; they are members of the family. This law recognizes that deep bond and ensures that pets' well-being and their relationship with both owners are considered, even after a separation. It's a forward-thinking move that shows how society's view of pets is evolving and becoming more inclusive in legal matters.

Now, for some truly heartbreaking news out of New York City. Police there have reported a tragic incident where a baby was shot dead. The seven-month-old child was in a pram when the shooting happened. Officials say that this tiny baby was an unintended victim. The shooting is suspected to be related to gang activity. This means the baby was not the target of the violence but sadly got caught in the crossfire. It's an incredibly sad story that highlights the devastating impact of gun violence, especially when innocent lives are lost. Our thoughts go out to the family of this young child. This kind of event reminds us of the serious challenges faced by communities dealing with such violence.

Finally, let's end on a more positive note with a story about recovery. A very old, very valuable golden helmet has been found. This ancient helmet was recovered more than a year after it was stolen during a heist in the Netherlands. The theft happened in January of 2025. Along with the helmet, three golden bracelets were also taken during this Dutch heist. While the good news is that the helmet is now safe, there's still one missing piece to this puzzle. One of those three golden bracelets is yet to be found. So, while we celebrate the return of this important historical artifact, the search continues for the last stolen item. It's a reminder that sometimes, even after a long time, valuable items can be brought back to their rightful place, even if the whole collection isn't complete yet. A significant recovery nonetheless!

And that brings us to the end of today's show. We covered a lot of ground, from the deep human impact of conflicts in Iran, to political shifts in the US, a celebrity lawsuit update, a compassionate new law for pets in Brazil, a tragic event in New York, and the exciting recovery of ancient treasure. Thank you for tuning in and joining me as we explored these stories. I hope you found today's discussion informative and thought-provoking. Until next time, stay safe, stay informed, and we'll talk again soon. Goodbye for now!
